beats.reasoning: Full energy start to a sunny Monday; Olivia wakes early to enjoy the weather forecast and plan her day around the Light Rail closure and 11am FIFA match.

beats.activity: Drive to Tukwila for first guitar lesson of the week
beats.reasoning: Commute from Kirkland to Tukwila workspace; early timing avoids mid-morning traffic and leaves room for the day's flexibility.

beats.activity: Teach private guitar lessons to students
beats.reasoning: Monday morning lesson block; Olivia typically schedules back-to-back students early in the week before energy dips.

beats.activity: Drive to Capitol Hill to catch USA vs Wales FIFA match
beats.reasoning: Heads to Capitol Hill bar to watch the 11am USA match; pragmatic choice to combine social energy with the world event rather than watch alone.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match at a bar, grab lunch
beats.reasoning: 11am match is a real-time event Olivia attends; bars downtown open early and she values social time; extends slightly for post-match conversation and food.

beats.activity: Walk around Capitol Hill, browse record store and local shops
beats.reasoning: Sunny day and high energy post-match; Olivia is curious by nature and loves exploring Capitol Hill's eclectic shops; avoids Light Rail closure (Westlake–U District closed 9am–3pm) by staying in Capitol Hill.

beats.activity: Drive back to Tukwila for afternoon lesson block
beats.reasoning: Return commute to teach more students in the afternoon; Light Rail closure is irrelevant since Olivia drives to Tukwila.

beats.activity: Teach afternoon and early-evening guitar lessons
beats.reasoning: Extended afternoon teaching block; Mondays are typically busy for private music instruction before the week gets fragmented.
Evening commute back to Kirkland after a full day of teaching; timing allows for relaxation before dinner.
Home meal after a long workday; Olivia uses this time to decompress and reset for the evening.

beats.activity: Relax with board games or practice guitar, listen to music
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening at home; Olivia enjoys solo creative time or light entertainment after a social and busy day; positioned before sleep beat.
Full 7.5-hour sleep cycle ending at 6am, allowing Olivia to wake refreshed for Tuesday; sleep beat spans midnight as required.

diary: Woke up to perfect early-June sunshine and couldn't resist the energy. Drove out to Tukwila for my morning lesson—one of my regulars finally nailed that fingerpicking pattern she's been working on for weeks. Made me smile. Then I caught myself checking the time and realized the World Cup match was happening, so I pivoted toward Capitol Hill instead. The bars were already packed by 10:15, and I found myself squeezed between a couple of rowdy fans at the counter with a sandwich I barely tasted. USA played okay. After, I wandered into my favorite record store on Pine Street, lost an hour flipping through vinyl. By afternoon I was back in Tukwila teaching—the routine felt grounding after all that spontaneity. Drove home as the light was turning golden. Made pasta, played some fingerstyle guitar on the porch while the day wound down. Quiet night. Felt balanced.
thought_process: The weather and the match created this unexpected pull toward the city—something I don't usually let myself do on a Monday. I almost skipped the Capitol Hill detour to stay focused on work, but I'm 48 and I've learned that life's too short to ignore those moments. The light rail closure didn't affect me since I drive, so I had flexibility the others might not have had. My students kept me grounded in the afternoon; teaching always centers me after I've been scattered. By evening I needed the quiet back home. The mix of structure (lessons) and spontaneity (the match, the record store) actually worked—I wasn't restless like I sometimes am on Mondays. The guitar at sunset sealed it.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
